subject: Mathematics
chapter: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables

key_points: A linear equation in two variables is of the form ax + by + c = 0
Such an equation represents a straight line on a graph
A pair of linear equations represents two straight lines
The solution of a pair of equations is the point of intersection of the lines
A pair of linear equations may have one solution, no solution, or infinitely many solutions
exam_tips: Always convert equations to standard form before comparing
Nature of solutions depends on coefficients of equations
Intersecting lines always have exactly one solution
